body_txt: This trope covers cases where someone, be it the intangible character themselves or another, tangible character, forgets that little detail and interacts with the intangible character in ways that would be possible if they where both (in)tangible, but isn't in the current situation. This is usually played for comedy and frequently results in someone or something crashing into the ground. The trope comes in two distinct flavors:  This variation of the trope is also frequently inverted in cases where a character who's used to being intangible becomes tangible again, resulting in them walking into a lot of walls.  A supertrope to Tragic Intangibility . Contrast Touch the Intangible , for cases where someone really can interact with the intangible. In cases where the intangibility also results in a person falling through the earth, see Required Secondary Powers . May be caused by a ghost not realizing they were Dead All Along .
